ideation: This sequence simulates the action of dribbling a basketball. The animation will focus on the right arm, which will mimic the motion of bouncing the ball, while the left arm remains relatively still for balance.  The animation should feel rhythmic and fluid, like a natural dribbling motion.  I will consult the single-frame and multi-frame examples to ensure the range of motion is realistic and the transitions between keyframes are significant. Frame 1 will show a neutral starting posture. Frame 2 will show the arm beginning the downward dribbling motion. Frame 3 will depict the lowest point of the dribble. Frame 4 will show the arm starting back up, and Frame 5 will show the arm returning to a near-neutral position, ready for the next dribble.

frames.beat: arms rest at sides, ready to dribble
right arm bends, preparing to push down
the ball is pushed towards the floor
the arm springs back up with the ball
right arm returns to ready position
